bullets.text: A powerful earthquake in Venezuela has led to at least 920 deaths, with one mother reportedly sacrificing her life to save her daughter. International rescue teams are arriving as anger grows over the official response and limited resources.

The Democratic Republic of Congo has accused Rwanda of various violations since the 1994 genocide, taking its neighbor to the international court. Meanwhile, concerns are rising over a significant Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, with nearly 300 infected individuals unaccounted for.

A Singapore-flagged ship in the Strait of Hormuz was attacked, leading to a pause in a large-scale evacuation. Following this incident, former US President Trump has accused Iran of breaching a ceasefire.

Australia's east coast is expecting heavy rain, continuing an un-El Ni

o-like weather pattern that has made 2026 the wettest on record for the country so far.
Australia's 15 native duck species are facing a new threat from the H5N1 bird flu, which has been discovered on Australian shores, adding to existing dangers like urban sprawl and climate change.
A Guardian Australia investigation revealed that the Christian Brothers sold real estate worth millions for only $1, despite claiming in court that they lack funds to compensate abuse survivors.
The US President has threatened 100% tariffs on countries implementing a digital services tax, indicating these levies would override existing trade deals.
Volkswagen is planning a major cost-cutting drive that could lead to up to 100,000 job cuts, representing nearly one in six workers.
Gold prices climbed above $4,000 an ounce after recent US inflation data dampened expectations for further interest-rate hikes.
